steps.title: Find the vertex input
steps.content:
The function $f(t)=51t-2t^2$ is a downward-opening parabola. Its maximum occurs at the vertex.
$$t=-\frac{b}{2a}$$
$$t=-\frac{51}{2(-2)}$$
$$t=\frac{51}{4}$$

steps.title: Add the vertical shift
steps.content:
Since $g(t)=f(t)+5$, the maximum of $g(t)$ is $5$ more than the maximum of $f(t)$.
$$5+2(\frac{51}{4})^2$$ The answer is B.
